themes.name: Historically bad run differential
themes.sample: Fans repeatedly citing worst NL run differential and being outscored 30-6 in three games as evidence the team is a disaster

themes.name: Offense is the core crisis
themes.sample: Broad consensus that the lineup is the real problem, not just Walker or any single pitcher

themes.name: Sanchez and bright pitching spots
themes.sample: Cristopher Sanchez praised for quality starts while Walker and Luzardo absorb heavy criticism

themes.name: Felix Reyes debut spark
themes.sample: First-career homer off Chris Sale generated the only genuine hype moment fans described all night

themes.name: Front office and coaching credibility collapsing
themes.sample: Fans mocking Dombrowski for hyping Kemp all offseason then optioning him after three weeks, and calling for Thomson's firing

quotes.text: Phillies management: Nah, let's run it back.
I hate to say it, but this team needs a rebuild.
quotes.source_hint: Comment on worst NL run differential post

quotes.text: Don't let Kevin Long get anywhere near him.
quotes.source_hint: Reaction to Reyes debut homer, fear of hitting coach ruining him

quotes.text: Sanchez has pitched his ass off and should by no means lose this game.

reasoning: Fan mood is at a near-crisis level driven by a historically poor run differential, an offense that ranks among the worst in baseball, and a deep skepticism that management or coaching will make meaningful changes. The lone bright spots — Cristopher Sanchez's continued quality pitching and Felix Reyes's storybook debut homer — generated genuine excitement but were quickly swallowed by the broader despair. Front office and managerial trust has eroded sharply, with fans questioning roster construction decisions and lineup choices made by Thomson, while Duran's IL stint registers mostly as dark comedy given the team rarely holds a lead to protect.
